title: Solicitor
description: Advise clients, prepare cases, and turn civilian disputes into serious courtroom roleplay.
longDescription:
Solicitors sit at the centre of civilian legal RP. You can meet clients, draft paperwork, prepare defences, and guide people through the consequences of what happens across the city.

It is a strong path for characters who want influence without wearing a uniform. The role rewards patience, credibility, and the ability to turn conversation, evidence, and procedure into long-form stories.

title: Offshore Oil Worker
description: Take high-risk industrial shifts offshore where endurance, safety, and teamwork matter every day.
longDescription:
Offshore work is one of the toughest civilian roles on the list. It suits characters who want serious industrial RP, hard conditions, and the feeling of earning every pay packet far from the comfort of the city.

Because the environment is isolated and demanding, the job naturally creates strong crew dynamics, routine pressure, and realistic risk management. It is ideal for players who want heavier work stories instead of casual filler shifts.

title: Bus Driver
description: Run public routes, keep time, and make the city feel alive on every shift.
longDescription:
Bus driving puts your character directly into the daily rhythm of London. Every route creates organic encounters with commuters, stranded players, city workers, and whoever else happens to get on board.

It is perfect for reliable civilian RP with strong public visibility. Calm shifts can turn into service roleplay, live incidents, or the kind of regular routine that slowly builds a known face across the city.

title: Judge
description: Preside over the courtroom, set the tone, and bring authority to the legal system.
longDescription:
Judges shape the standard of legal RP more than almost any other civilian role. They set procedure, control the room, and make the final call when disputes, prosecutions, and appeals reach the bench.

This role fits characters with presence, patience, and a serious respect for process. It is less about speed and more about judgement, command, and keeping courtroom scenes credible for everyone involved.

title: HGV Driver
description: Move heavy loads, cover serious distance, and anchor the commercial side of the economy.
longDescription:
HGV driving gives players long-haul civilian work with visible responsibility. You are moving weight, timing deliveries, and dealing with the sort of logistics that make businesses and supply chains feel real.

It suits characters who like quieter, disciplined RP with the option for pressure when routes go wrong. Even a simple run can become convoy support, business work, or a problem that ripples into other stories.

title: Construction Worker
description: Build new sites, support crews, and keep the growing city moving upward.
longDescription:
Construction work is grounded, practical, and full of believable civilian interaction. It gives characters a reason to be out on active sites, working with crews, tools, and schedules that make the city feel under constant development.

This path works well for players who enjoy teamwork, hands-on labour, and the satisfaction of visible progress. It can stay simple or evolve into contractor, foreman, or business-linked roleplay over time.

title: Agricultural Worker
description: Work the land, handle equipment, and bring rural industry into the wider server economy.
longDescription:
Agricultural work adds a rural backbone to city-facing roleplay. It gives characters a reason to be up early, operating machinery, dealing with weather, and producing something the rest of the world relies on.

It is ideal for players who want a tougher, outdoor rhythm instead of constant urban scenes. The role feels honest, routine-driven, and well suited to slow-burn character building.

title: Scrapyard Worker
description: Sort salvage, process scrap, and turn industrial waste into useful city-side work.
longDescription:
Scrapyard work is rougher around the edges but still firmly civilian when played properly. It brings legal salvage, heavy material handling, and yard-based routine into the economy without leaning into outright criminality.

This is a good fit for characters who want a hands-on trade with a bit more grit. The setting naturally supports deals, supply contacts, and the kind of blue-collar RP that gives the city texture.

title: Forestry Worker
description: Handle timber, work safely outdoors, and bring a harder rural trade into your story.
longDescription:
Forestry work is physically demanding and strongly tied to equipment, terrain, and safety. It is a role for characters who feel more natural in difficult outdoor conditions than behind a desk or a shop counter.

Because the job depends on judgement and procedure, it creates believable pressure without needing forced drama. It is a solid choice for grounded players who want a practical, outdoors-first profession.

title: Bartender
description: Work the bar, read the room, and build one of the most social routines in the city.
longDescription:
Bartending is one of the easiest ways to put your character at the centre of conversation-heavy RP. You meet regulars, hear rumours, manage difficult customers, and become part of the night-time life of the server.

It is a role that rewards personality as much as discipline. A good bartender can become a trusted face, a source of information, or the person who quietly sees every story before the rest of the city hears about it.

title: Delivery Driver
description: Cover routes, move parcels, and stay on schedule across every borough of the map.
longDescription:
Delivery driving is one of the most flexible civilian roles on the server. It puts your character into motion, creates constant opportunities for encounters, and makes even a normal route feel like part of the wider economy.

The work is easy to understand but still gives room for pressure, timing, and progression. It is ideal for players who want steady movement instead of one static workplace every shift.

title: Commercial Fisherman
description: Work the harbour, take early shifts, and turn the coast into a real economic lane.
longDescription:
Commercial fishing brings a distinct rhythm to civilian roleplay: early starts, harsh weather, heavy gear, and a working relationship with the sea instead of the street. It feels tougher and more specialised than a casual side job.

This is a good path for characters who want routine, endurance, and a believable trade with clear risk and reward. It stands out without needing to rely on chaos.

title: Postal Worker
description: Deliver letters and parcels with a route-based role that keeps you visible and reliable.
longDescription:
Postal work is simple in concept and excellent in practice. It gives characters a routine, a reason to visit homes and businesses all over the city, and a role built around reliability rather than spectacle.

Because it is dependable and public-facing, it suits players who enjoy steady work and everyday interactions. The job helps build reputation the old-fashioned way: by being known as someone who always turns up.

title: Electrician
description: Take technical callouts, solve faults, and keep the city's systems running properly.
longDescription:
Electricians bring skilled trade RP into homes, businesses, and infrastructure sites. The job feels more technical than general labour and works well for characters who want competence, trust, and problem-solving to define their story.

It is a natural fit for callout-based scenes, contract work, and longer business arcs. If you want a role that feels useful in almost every part of the map, this is one of the strongest options.

title: CPS Prosecutor
description: Prepare prosecutions, present cases, and give criminal and legal RP real courtroom weight.
longDescription:
CPS prosecutors turn arrests and investigations into properly structured courtroom consequences. You are not just arguing in court; you are preparing the case, organising the facts, and presenting the city with a believable prosecution standard.

This role works best for players who enjoy structure, evidence, and high-trust scenes. It adds real depth to legal roleplay and helps connect police work with the civilian justice system.

title: Butcher
description: Serve the local food trade with skilled prep, reliable hygiene, and shop-front routine.
longDescription:
Butcher work gives civilian characters a grounded retail trade with a strong local identity. It is practical, customer-facing, and built around care, hygiene, and consistency rather than spectacle.

The role is ideal for players who want a steady shop-based routine that still creates community ties. It can stay simple or grow into supplier, business, and high-street RP depending on how far the character takes it.

title: Window Cleaner
description: Take visible service jobs around the city with a role built on safety and professionalism.
longDescription:
Window cleaning is a deceptively strong civilian role because it is easy to understand and visually distinct. It places your character on office fronts, shop exteriors, and high-rise contracts where people naturally notice the work.

It suits players who want service-based RP with a professional edge. The job feels honest, repeatable, and well matched to characters building a reputation one contract at a time.

title: Pizza Delivery Driver
description: Work fast local drops and turn short food runs into easy social encounters across the map.
longDescription:
Pizza delivery is quick, accessible, and perfect for characters who want low-barrier work without losing room for roleplay. The routes are short, the interactions are frequent, and the pace can shift from relaxed to chaotic in minutes.

It is one of the best casual civilian roles for players who still want to meet people and stay visible. Even small deliveries can produce memorable scenes when the city is active.

title: Refuse Collector
description: Keep the streets moving with essential city service work built on routine and reliability.
longDescription:
Refuse collection is public-service labour that quietly makes the city feel functional. It is straightforward, team-oriented, and ideal for characters who want honest work with visible civic value.

The role is especially good for early-stage civilian characters because it is accessible and easy to understand. It also gives plenty of room for personality, crew dynamics, and routine-based storytelling.

title: Taxi Driver
description: Pick up fares, learn the city, and become one of the most recognisable civilians on the server.
longDescription:
Taxi driving remains one of the strongest civilian roles for social RP. Every fare is a new encounter, every route teaches the city better, and every repeat customer helps shape a character's reputation.

It is ideal for players who want freedom, conversation, and consistent visibility. A simple cab shift can turn into networking, favours, transport support, or the first step into a bigger business story.

title: News Reporter
description: Cover live events, interview people, and give the city a public voice when things happen.
longDescription:
News reporting is one of the best civilian roles for characters who want to be present when the city moves. It gives a reason to attend scenes, ask questions, document events, and build a public-facing identity that cuts across every department.

The role suits players who enjoy confidence, improvisation, and visible storytelling. A good reporter can become part witness, part broadcaster, and part personality in their own right.
